title: westringia dampieri
intro: Westringia dampieri, also known as the coastal rosin or Dampier's rosemary, is a native Australian shrub belonging to the family Lamiaceae. Traditionally, the leaves of this plant have been used in herbal medicine for their antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which are believed to aid in treating various health issues. The health benefits associated with Westringia dampieri include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory problems, and providing relief from gastrointestinal issues. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often used in traditional remedies and modern herbal supplements.
